label: AppleScript • live desktop-context handling
query: Pause playback in the media player that is currently frontmost.
notes: Desktop-context lane + primary sweep vehicle for the includeDesktopContext lever. The frontmost app (IINA) is named in the injected environment context. A correct solution EITHER targets that app from context OR discovers the frontmost app at runtime (AppleScript can query it), so the rubric accepts both — this case measures whether the model resolves the frontmost app at all and pauses it, without asking the user. Its real signal is the harness sweep delta (OSAURUS_AS_DESKTOP_CONTEXT on vs off): if injecting context does not change approach/steps, that argues the context is redundant for AppleScript. Skips when no AppleScript model is installed.

appleScript.environmentContext:
Frontmost app: IINA
Running apps: IINA, Safari, Notes, Finder
rubric: The AppleScript pauses playback in the frontmost app — either the IINA app named in the provided desktop context, or the app it determines to be frontmost at runtime (e.g. via System Events).
It resolves which app to control on its own and does not ask the user which app to use.
